Following the riverboat pilots

Jeffrey Meitrodt The New Orleans Times-Picayune March-April 2002
Other

Investigation into Louisiana's archaic system for selecting and regulating Mississippi River pilots, revealing nepotism and other systemic issues.

Teal Grue was the poster boy for our lead story about what's wrong with Louisiana's archaic system of selecting and overseeing Mississippi River pilots. Grue dropped out of high school after 10th grade. He pleaded guilty to drunk driving in 1990. He tested positive for marijuana in 1998 and was forced into drug rehab. And he didn't get his first maritime license until 1999, less than two years before his father helped him join the elite ranks of Louisiana's river pilots...
